explanation: This command simulates a potential abuse scenario by using the 'executecmd64' parameter to execute a command. It aligns with the Sigma rule's detection of 'executecmd64' in the command line.
This command uses the '--invokeexe' parameter to launch an application, which is another string the Sigma rule detects. It demonstrates a safe mutation by using long-form syntax.
This command uses the 'injectDll' parameter to simulate DLL injection behavior, which is flagged by the Sigma rule. It represents a direct match to the rule's detection criteria.
This command adds extra spaces around the '-executecmd64' parameter to test whether the Sigma rule is resilient to spacing mutations.
This command uses an equals sign to pass the DLL path to the 'injectDll' parameter, testing equivalent syntax variations.
